steps.title: Start your own delivery service in 4 steps
steps.subtitle: From idea to first order — what you really need

items.title: Set up your ordering system
items.text: This is where you decide: commission forever — or 0% from now on? With Gastro Master you get an online shop, app and POS system. No prior knowledge needed, live in 2–3 weeks.
items.highlight: 0% commission from the first order. Personal setup assistance.

items.title: Choose a niche — don't offer everything
items.text: Focus beats a broad offering. A delivery service for vegan bowls wins regular customers faster than an all-rounder. Start with one cuisine category.
items.highlight: Recommendation: 1 cuisine category, 8–12 dishes, 1 delivery area.

items.title: Choose a business model
items.text: In-house production, ghost kitchen (kitchen without dining room) or intermediary? A ghost kitchen saves 60–80% on rent.
items.highlight: Ghost kitchen: 60–80% less rent than a traditional restaurant

items.title: Register your business
items.text: Business registration costs €20–60 and takes just a few days. You also need hygiene training (HACCP) and a health certificate. No restaurant license needed.
items.highlight: Cost: €20–60. Duration: 1–2 weeks. No driver's license needed.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about starting a delivery service
faq.subtitle: The questions founders ask Google and AI assistants

items.q: Is starting your own delivery service worth it in 2026?
items.a: Yes — according to Statista, the market is growing from €6.1B (2023) to €9.9B by 2028. Those who build their own system early stop paying commission. With a [digital ordering system for gastronomy](/produkte/webshop) you start from €79/month — instead of up to 30% on every order.

items.q: How much does it cost to start a delivery service?
items.a: That depends on how you want to start. Business registration costs €20–60. For a ghost kitchen (kitchen without dining room), startup costs from €2,000–5,000 are realistic. The ordering system — [online shop](/produkte/webshop) and [your own ordering app](/produkte/app) — costs from €79/month. You save the most money in ongoing operations: no fees of 13–30% on every order. Find all costs in the [complete pricing overview](/preise).

items.q: Do I need a restaurant to start a delivery service?
items.a: No. You can start as a ghost kitchen — a kitchen without a dining room, only for deliveries. This saves a lot of rent and makes starting cheaper. You only need a business registration and must comply with food hygiene regulations. If you have an existing restaurant, find suitable solutions on the [restaurant page](/loesungen/restaurant).

items.q: How much commission does Lieferando charge?
items.a: Lieferando charges 13% commission if you have your own drivers. With Lieferando drivers, it goes up to 30% per order. Wolt and Uber Eats charge similarly at 15–30%. Source: Berliner Abendblatt. With your [own delivery service without commission](/loesungen/lieferdienst) you pay a fixed monthly fee instead — no deductions on every order.

items.q: What permits do I need for a delivery service?
items.a: You need: (1) Business registration at the trade office (€20–60), (2) Hygiene training (HACCP) — available online, approx. €20–50, (3) Health certificate for everyone who works with food (approx. €30). You don't need a restaurant license if you only deliver. Our team advises you for free — just contact us via the [contact form](/kontakt).
